title: draba hirta var. alpicola
intro: Draba hirta var. alpicola, also known as Alpine Draba, is a rare, alpine plant species belonging to the mustard family, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic, helping to reduce water retention and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract issues. The health benefits of Alpine Draba include its potential to reduce inflammation, lower blood pressure, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tea form, as well as in salves and ointments, to take advantage of its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
